beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee, review the day ahead.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning wake-up; low energy yesterday (35/100) means a slower start and time to recharge at home before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive to Green Lake for a morning walk and light exercise.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny weather and low energy from yesterday suggest an outdoor recharge; Green Lake is close to Greenwood and offers a peaceful start to Sunday.

beats.activity: Walk around the lake, stretch, and enjoy the June light.
beats.reasoning: Restorative outdoor time on a calm, sunny day; Nathan values quiet moments and this fits his mood-based planning style.
Return home before lunch; short drive from Green Lake to Greenwood.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a healthy lunch at home.
beats.reasoning: Sunday lunch at home; Nathan is budget-conscious and organized, so cooking at home aligns with his spreadsheet-tracking personality.

beats.activity: Review grant spreadsheets and expenses from the week.
beats.reasoning: Nathan keeps meticulous expense spreadsheets; Sunday is a natural day for weekly review and planning.

beats.activity: Paint and work on personal art project.
beats.reasoning: Nathan spends free time painting; Sunday afternoon is ideal for creative work after a restorative morning.

beats.activity: Drive to a close friend's place in Capitol Hill for board game night prep.
beats.reasoning: Nathan values time with close friends over big crowds; a Sunday board game night is a regular social anchor.

beats.activity: Board game night with close friends.
beats.reasoning: Nathan prioritizes board games and close friendships; a long, relaxed Sunday evening gathering fits his personality and social values.
Return home after social time; Capitol Hill to Greenwood is about 20–25 min on Sunday evening with light traffic.

beats.activity: Make a simple dinner and eat while reviewing notes for the week ahead.
beats.reasoning: Organized Sunday routine; Nathan plans his week and keeps notes on grant work, so evening prep is typical.

beats.activity: Wind down with reading and light stretching.
beats.reasoning: Sunday evening relaxation; Nathan values quiet time and needs a buffer before sleep after a full day.
Eight and a half hours of sleep to recharge for the week ahead; ends before 06:30 AM as required.

diary: Woke up early enough to catch the sunrise over coffee—one of those perfect Seattle Sundays where the light just feels generous. Drove out to Green Lake and walked the whole loop, letting the water and trees do their thing. My legs felt strong today, which was nice. Back home I tackled the grant spreadsheets from the week, trying to make sense of where we spent what, then lost myself in my painting for a bit. The colors weren't cooperating, but I wasn't frustrated about it. Headed to Capitol Hill for board games with the crew—we demolished each other at Ticket to Ride, and I somehow won a round of Codenames. The afternoon light streaming through their apartment windows felt like a small gift. Drove home as the city cooled down, made a simple dinner, and now I'm sitting here feeling that Sunday-evening kind of quiet. Tomorrow the work week starts again.
thought_process: I needed today to be restorative and it was. The morning walk wasn't just exercise—it was me resetting my mind before a week of chasing deadlines and budgets. I've been running hard on grant proposals, so I deliberately kept the day loose, letting myself move between things without pressure. The board games were the right call; I almost talked myself out of going, but my friends have a way of pulling me back into the present moment. I chose to review my work notes during dinner rather than during the social time because I knew I'd obsess otherwise. The painting didn't need to be perfect today. Everything felt balanced—work, movement, creativity, people. The weather helped too; those 72-degree days don't come every week, and I wasn't going to waste it inside.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
